title: Rewrite security-checklist.md for Clojure/JVM + CLJS
description: Current checklist covers Astro/Vercel: PUBLIC_ env vars, Mux signing, Vercel headers, npm audit. Rewrite for this stack: JVM secrets/env, SSRF on the configurable ingest URL, unsafe read-string/EDN, Reagent XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML, CORS on the SSE endpoint, MapLibre tile-provider key handling, and Clojure dependency auditing.

title: Rewrite validation-boundaries.md around the ingest boundary
description: Current doc says the stack has essentially no trust boundaries (static site, no forms, no API). That is now false: ultrafeeder's aircraft.json is the primary untrusted input, plus the HTTP API and SSE stream. Build the doc around Malli schemas in the shared .cljc domain validating at ingest.

title: Internet-exposure hardening: TLS, headers, SSE limits
description: The app is internet-facing, which is beyond what docs currently assume. Reverse proxy with TLS in front of http-kit, security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options), a cap on concurrent SSE connections and per-IP limits so anonymous browsers cannot exhaust the server, and an update to security-checklist.md and validation-boundaries.md to reflect the new exposure in the same change.
acceptance_criteria: TLS terminates at the reverse proxy; CSP, HSTS, and X-Content-Type-Options verified on responses; a concurrent-SSE cap and per-IP limit are enforced and tested; security-checklist.md and validation-boundaries.md are updated in the same change.
notes: PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): treat the receiver position as a server-side secret - record it as such in validation-boundaries.md / security-checklist.md when updating them for internet exposure. Also ensure /data endpoints of the feeder itself are not proxied through.

title: Emergency squawk alerting (7700 / 7600 / 7500)
description: The alerting path the docs mandate and the squawking-7700 cast member exercises: emergency? as a pure cljc domain fn, unmissable map treatment via style expressions, a badge in the list and detail panel, and prominence in the chrome while any emergency is active. A plane squawking 7700 is a human being having the worst day of their life - the UI must not whisper it.
acceptance_criteria: emergency? is a pure cljc fn tested for 7700, 7600, and 7500; the squawking-7700 fixture triggers the map treatment and badges in list and panel; the chrome makes an active emergency impossible to miss.

title: geo.cljc: bearing, distance, bounds + aircraft-to-GeoJSON
description: The pure shared geo module the README promises: bearing, distance, bounds math, and conversion of the aircraft picture into a GeoJSON FeatureCollection with the properties the map styling needs (track, altitude, emergency, stale). Pure cljc, no map dependency, fully unit-tested against the fixture cast.
acceptance_criteria: Bearing, distance, and bounds are unit-tested against known values; the fixture cast converts to a FeatureCollection carrying track, altitude, emergency, and stale properties; never-positioned yields no feature; pure cljc with no MapLibre dependency.

title: Imperative aircraft layer via GeoJSON setData
description: The architectural centerpiece: a MapLibre GeoJSON source updated via setData on every SSE tick, entirely outside React. Hundreds of aircraft at 1 Hz must not touch the Reagent render path. re-frame owns the chrome; the map owns the planes. Includes the interop seam that lets tests fake the map object per testing-setup.md.
acceptance_criteria: Aircraft updates flow through setData with zero Reagent re-renders (asserted in a test via render counting); the fake-map seam lets CLJS tests capture setData payloads; hundreds of aircraft at 1 Hz stay smooth.

title: SSE client with reconnect and connection state
description: EventSource wrapper: parse snapshot and update events into app-db aircraft state, reconnect with backoff when the stream drops, and expose connection status (live / reconnecting / down) as a re-frame sub so the chrome can show it honestly. The map should recover from a server restart without a page reload.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot and update events land in app-db; killing the server flips the connection sub to reconnecting and recovery is automatic without a page reload; reconnect attempts back off.

title: App shell: shadow-cljs app build, MapLibre init, re-frame skeleton
description: index.html, the shadow-cljs :app build wired into bb dev, re-frame app-db initialization, and a MapLibre map mounted with a basemap style (choose a no-token style source - the docs promise no browser-visible secrets) with correct attribution. The empty map that everything else lands on.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev serves the app; the map renders a basemap with correct attribution and no vendor token anywhere in the bundle; re-frame app-db initializes; the CLJS suite can mount the shell in headless Chromium.
notes: PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): default map center must be a fixed general region (e.g. the coverage area), never the receiver position.

title: SSE fan-out of aircraft state
description: The stream the browser lives on: on connect, send a full snapshot of the current picture, then push updates at ~1 Hz. Defines the wire format (JSON from the domain model - decide and document how namespaced keys serialize). Client registry with cleanup on disconnect, and a policy for slow consumers so one stalled browser cannot back-pressure the loop or leak channels.
acceptance_criteria: A new client receives a full snapshot then ~1 Hz updates; disconnect removes the client without leaking channels; a slow-consumer policy is implemented and documented; periodic SSE heartbeat comments keep the stream alive through buffering proxies; the wire format is documented.
notes: PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): receiver position is private. The wire format must exclude the receiver position AND all receiver-relative fields (r_dst, r_dir) - they are trivially invertible to the antenna location. Add a test asserting these fields never appear in SSE payloads.

title: HTTP server: reitit routes, Malli coercion, static assets
description: http-kit server with reitit routing: /api/aircraft/:icao with Malli-coerced path params and schema-checked responses per validation-boundaries.md Boundary 2, a health endpoint for the container orchestrator, static serving of the compiled frontend, and a start/stop lifecycle that is REPL-friendly. No hand-rolled parsing in handlers; no keyword interning of user input.
acceptance_criteria: A malformed :icao is rejected by Malli coercion with a 400 before any handler code runs; responses are schema-checked; the health endpoint reports feeder reachability; the compiled frontend is served; start/stop is idempotent from the REPL.

title: Aircraft state store + staleness domain
description: The current picture of the sky. Pure cljc domain fns in adsb.aircraft: merge an incoming batch into the picture keyed by ICAO hex, stale? by seen-age with time passed as an argument (never read the clock in cljc), and age-out removal of long-silent aircraft. Backend holds the picture in one atom updated by the poll loop. The long-silent and never-positioned cast members are the tests.
acceptance_criteria: Pure cljc: merging a batch updates the picture keyed by hex; stale? and age-out take time as an argument, never reading a clock; the long-silent cast member ages out and never-positioned is retained without a position; the backend atom is updated only by the poll loop.

title: Source protocol + ultrafeeder polling source
description: adsb.ingest.source/Source protocol and its first implementation: poll /data/aircraft.json at ~1 Hz. ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL comes from the environment only, is validated at boot (must parse, must be http/https, fail loudly at startup - see validation-boundaries.md Boundary 3). The loop backs off when the feeder is unreachable and never dies; a feeder outage is a status, not a crash.
acceptance_criteria: Boot fails loudly on a missing, malformed, or non-http(s) ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL; the poll loop survives a feeder outage with visible backoff and recovers when the feeder returns; the Source protocol has a second trivial implementation (test stub) proving the seam; ingest tests replay the recorded fixture, never a live feeder.
notes: Real feeder: http://dietpi.local:8100 (ultrafeeder on dietpi.local). SBS on 30003 and Beast on 30005, same host, for the future Source swap.

title: Frontend map core
description: The browser half: a MapLibre map that renders live aircraft pushed over SSE. The load-bearing rule from AUTEUR and the README applies throughout - the aircraft layer bypasses React entirely and is fed via GeoJSON setData; re-frame owns only the chrome.
acceptance_criteria: Open the app and watch live aircraft move, styled by altitude and heading, aging out honestly - with the 1 Hz path never touching React.

title: Streaming and HTTP API
description: The seam between backend and browser: an http-kit + reitit server with Malli-coerced routes, and the SSE fan-out that pushes the aircraft picture to every connected browser at ~1 Hz. Boundary 2 of docs/validation-boundaries.md lives here.
acceptance_criteria: A browser connects and receives the live picture over SSE; every HTTP surface is Malli-coerced at the edge and schema-checked on the way out.

title: Ingest pipeline and live aircraft state
description: The backend half of the data flow, downstream of the validation boundary built in adsb-bvi.3: poll the ultrafeeder behind the Source protocol, keep the poll loop alive through feeder outages, and maintain the current picture of the sky - a state store keyed by ICAO hex that ages out silent aircraft. Ends where the SSE stream begins.
acceptance_criteria: A running backend maintains an accurate current picture of the sky from a real feeder, survives outages and malformed entries, and flags implausible data instead of trusting or hiding it.

title: Malli schemas + ingest boundary
description: src/cljc/adsb/schema.cljc and the ingest path per docs/validation-boundaries.md: coerce alt_baro (number OR the string 'ground'), keep absent fields absent rather than defaulting to 0, trim space-padded callsigns, drop never-positioned aircraft, enforce plausibility limits separately from schema validity, and guarantee one malformed aircraft cannot kill the batch or the poll loop.
acceptance_criteria: alt_baro accepts a number or the string ground; absent fields stay absent - no zero defaults; callsigns are trimmed; never-positioned aircraft are dropped at the boundary; plausibility limits are enforced separately from schema validity; a batch with one malformed entry yields the rest plus one log line, and the poll loop survives.
notes: From the real capture (adsb-bvi.2): alt_baro is ABSENT on ~13% of observations (bare mode_s targets carry only hex/messages/rssi/seen) - the schema must treat alt_baro as optional, not just number-or-ground. Fixture: test/resources/aircraft-sample.json (51 aircraft, warts preserved byte-exact).

title: Record a real aircraft.json payload as a test fixture
description: Capture a genuine payload from the ultrafeeder and commit it to test/resources/aircraft-sample.json — warts included: the 'ground' altitude strings, the aircraft with no lat/lon, the space-padded callsigns, the nulls. Hand-written fixtures are a fiction of what we THINK the feeder sends; a recording is what it does send. Everything in docs/validation-boundaries.md is asserted from knowledge of readsb/tar1090's format, NOT from looking at this specific feeder's output — so verify each claimed quirk against the real capture and correct the doc if any is wrong.
acceptance_criteria: A committed sample payload; each quirk listed in the validation-boundaries.md table either confirmed present or the doc corrected.
notes:
Feeder confirmed live at http://dietpi.local:8100/data/aircraft.json (2026-07-11).

FIXTURE CAPTURED 2026-07-11: 10 polls at ~25s intervals, 49-52 aircraft each (348+ aircraft-observations). Richest capture (poll 10, 51 aircraft) committed as test/resources/aircraft-sample.json. PRIVACY REDACTION: r_dst and r_dir stripped from every aircraft (39 of 51 had them) via byte-preserving text surgery; verified JSON-equal to original minus exactly those keys; no receiver lat/lon fields existed in the payload. Provenance documented in test/resources/README.md.

TRAP TABLE VERIFICATION (against all 10 captures):
- alt_baro number: CONFIRMED (abc0e4 alt_baro=34775). Only int seen; no floats.
- alt_baro string 'ground': NOT OBSERVED in capture window (no airport-surface traffic in range). Claim stands, uncontradicted.
- lat/lon absent entirely: CONFIRMED (a10202, abe075, ad072c in fixture; 102 occurrences total).
- flight space-padded to 8 chars: CONFIRMED (ac5697 'SWA349  ', a5d4c3 'GTI9071 '; 397 occurrences). Also flight absent: CONFIRMED (abc0e4, adc9b6).
- hex ~-prefix (TIS-B/ADS-R): NOT OBSERVED. Only types seen: adsb_icao (292), mode_s (56). Uncontradicted.
- squawk string of 4 octal digits: CONFIRMED, always string, never int (a49498 '0745', a4008e '0541' — leading zeros prove type matters; 309 occurrences). '0000' itself not seen.
- gs/track/baro_rate absent: CONFIRMED (ac5b89 missing all three; 79/79/127 occurrences).
- null values: NOT OBSERVED (0 nulls across all captures). Claim is 'may be null' — uncontradicted.
- BONUS quirk not in table: alt_baro itself absent on 45/348 observations (abe075, a97310) — mode_s aircraft often carry only hex/messages/rssi.

NO DOC CORRECTIONS NEEDED: no trap-table claim was contradicted by the data.
close_reason: 51-aircraft real capture committed-ready at test/resources/ with r_dst/r_dir redacted (verified 0 occurrences); all trap-table quirks confirmed or explicitly not-observed; no doc corrections needed.

title: Bootstrap the project skeleton
description: The doc set now describes a project in detail — build commands, source layout, namespaces, schemas — but NONE of it exists. Not a single .clj file, no deps.edn, no bb.edn. Stand up the skeleton the docs promise, and correct the docs wherever reality disagrees.
acceptance_criteria: The skeleton the docs promise exists and every documented command runs; the real feeder payload is captured and the schemas hold against it; the fixture cast is live.

title: deps.edn, bb.edn, shadow-cljs.edn + source tree
description: Create the build surface the docs assume: deps.edn (Ring/reitit, http-kit, Malli, cheshire), shadow-cljs.edn (:app and :test builds), bb.edn (dev, test, test:clj, test:cljs, lint, build), and the src/{clj,cljc,cljs}/adsb + test/ tree. Every command named in README.md and CLAUDE.md must actually run.
acceptance_criteria: Every command named in README.md and CLAUDE.md runs: bb dev, bb test, bb test:clj, bb test:cljs, bb lint, bb build; the src/{clj,cljc,cljs}/adsb and mirrored test/ tree exists; bb lint passes clean on the skeleton.
notes: Build surface created and verified. deps.edn (clojure 1.12.1, http-kit 2.8.0, reitit 0.7.2, malli 0.17.0, cheshire 5.13.0, tools.logging+logback; aliases :dev/:test/:lint), shadow-cljs.edn (:app browser build, :test browser-test build with runner-ns adsb.test-runner, dev-http 8290), package.json (maplibre-gl, react 18, shadow-cljs 2.28.20), bb.edn tasks dev/test/test:clj/test:cljs/test:watch/lint/build. Skeleton ns: adsb.dev (http-kit stub on 8280), adsb.aircraft (pure cljc stale?), adsb.core (cljs init!), mirrored tests pass on JVM; cljc+cljs compile in the :test build. clj-kondo runs as a lib via clojure -M:lint (no binary needed). All six bb commands verified exit 0; bb dev boots backend+shadow watch and was curl-checked on :8280. CLAUDE.md test:cljs line corrected: compile-only until adsb-4ga.8. Not committed (conservative profile).

title: Build and verify the CLJS browser test runner
description: docs/testing-setup.md specifies the intended CLJS test setup but NOTHING IN IT HAS BEEN RUN — there is no code in the repo yet. Build it and prove it works: shadow-cljs :browser-test target, a custom runner ns that signals completion and failure count to the page, a Playwright script that compiles, serves, drives the page, and maps results to an exit code, and a 'bb test:cljs' task wrapping it. Verify the exact shadow-cljs runner API against the real version; the doc's code is directionally right but unverified. Correct testing-setup.md wherever reality disagrees with it.
acceptance_criteria: bb test runs clj + cljc + cljs suites; a deliberately failing cljs test produces a non-zero exit code; a Reagent component renders and responds to a React event under React Testing Library; day8.re-frame.test isolates app-db between tests.
notes: Left for this bead by adsb-bvi.1: bb test:cljs currently only compiles the :test build (npx shadow-cljs compile test) and prints a note — no browser execution, no failure exit code from actual test runs yet. Already in place for you: shadow-cljs.edn :test build (:browser-test target, test-dir target/browser-test, ns-regexp -test$, runner-ns adsb.test-runner), test/cljs/adsb/test_runner.cljs parking results on window.adsbTestResult/adsbTestsDone per docs/testing-setup.md, :dev-http {8290 target/browser-test}, and a bb test:watch task. Still needed: Playwright dep + script/run-browser-tests.mjs, wiring bb test:cljs to serve the page and drive Chromium, verifying a failing test exits non-zero, and correcting testing-setup.md (incl. its Status note) plus the CLAUDE.md test:cljs comment (currently says compile-only, ref adsb-4ga.8).

title: Rewrite archetypes (AUTEUR, BOB, PENNY) for the Clojure stack
description: All three personas reference Astro/React/Vitest/Mux and web/src paths. AUTEUR becomes the CLJS/Reagent/re-frame/MapLibre owner with an explicit rule that the high-churn aircraft layer bypasses React. BOB's 'framework conventions win' section becomes Clojure idiom. PENNY's security surface becomes the ingest/API/JVM one. Also drop AUTEUR's dangling refs to /docs/design-direction.md and /docs/design-questionnaire.md, which do not exist.

title: Rewrite testing docs for clojure.test + cljs.test
description: testing-standards.md is mostly framework-agnostic but assumes BDD string test names; testing-setup.md is entirely Vitest/AstroContainer/React Testing Library. Retarget to clojure.test/cljs.test via reader conditionals, shadow-cljs test targets, bb task surface, re-frame testing, MapLibre interop faking, and Malli generators for property tests.
notes: DECISION (Overseer, after correcting me): clojure.test/cljs.test + shadow-cljs :browser-test target + Playwright driving the generated test page. NOT Karma — Karma was EOL'd in 2023 and I wrongly recommended it. NOT jsdom — no layout engine, no WebGL, so MapLibre cannot init and getBoundingClientRect returns zeros. Overseer noted c3kit.scaffold gives speclj a ready-made Playwright runner; we chose clojure.test anyway for ecosystem reach (day8.re-frame.test is built on cljs.test/async and works natively), accepting that we own the Playwright plumbing ourselves. Fidelity is identical either way — both drive a real browser process.

title: Rewrite docs/clean-code-standards.md for Clojure/CLJS
description: Currently 572 lines of Astro/TypeScript guidance (arrow functions, trailing commas, PascalCase components, ESM imports). Rewrite for Clojure idiom: kebab-case, ?/! suffixes, threading macros, destructuring, ns organization, data over objects. Keep the structure, philosophy, and agent-friendliness sections.

title: Write README.md
description: No README exists. Write a simple one: what ADSB is, the stack, quickstart, project layout, and pointers into docs/ and archetypes/.

title: Retarget agent doc set from Astro/TS to Clojure/CLJS
description: The repo's docs/ and archetypes/ were carried over from a static Astro+TypeScript portfolio site (Vercel, Mux, GSAP, Vitest, web/src paths). Every file must be rewritten for this project's actual stack: full-stack Clojure + ClojureScript with a shared .cljc domain, ingesting live ADS-B from a local ultrafeeder and rendering a live aircraft map.
design: Stack decided with the Overseer: deps.edn + babashka + shadow-cljs; Ring/reitit + http-kit backend; Malli for schema/validation in shared .cljc; clojure.test/cljs.test via reader conditionals; Reagent + re-frame for UI chrome; MapLibre GL JS for the map with the aircraft layer updated imperatively via GeoJSON setData (bypassing React); ingest by polling ultrafeeder /data/aircraft.json behind a swappable protocol; SSE for server->client state push.
acceptance_criteria: Every file in docs/ and archetypes/ describes this project truthfully, and the one executable claim - the CLJS browser test runner in testing-setup.md - has been proven by running it.
notes: All 7 doc files written (README, 5 docs, 3 archetypes, CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md). Epic stays OPEN deliberately: docs/testing-setup.md specifies a CLJS browser-test runner that has never been executed, because there is no code in the repo yet. The epic is not honestly 'done' until adsb-4ga.8 proves that doc correct. Everything else in the doc set is prose and can stand on its own.

title: Go-live: provision host, DNS, first deploy, smoke check
description: The last mile that no other bead owns: provision the cloud host, point DNS at it, obtain the TLS cert, run the first real deploy, and smoke-check the full path - antenna to ultrafeeder to tunnel to backend to SSE to a phone on cellular data. The testing docs forbid live-feeder tests; this is not a test, it is the operational acceptance of the whole system.
acceptance_criteria: The app is reachable at its public HTTPS domain from off-network; live aircraft render and move; the connection indicator behaves through a deliberate tunnel bounce; a runbook section in the README covers redeploy and rollback.
notes: Overseer 2026-07-11: host will probably be a DigitalOcean droplet. Domain DECIDED: map.bwawan.com (subdomain of the Overseer's bwawan.com). If bwawan.com DNS lands on Cloudflare, TLS and the cloudflared tunnel share one ecosystem - verify where the zone is hosted during this bead.

title: Production basemap tile source
description: The dev map can lean on a free demo style, but MapLibre demo tiles are explicitly not for production, and this app will be internet-facing. Pick a sustainable, no-token tile source - OpenFreeMap, self-hosted PMTiles/Protomaps, or similar - verify its usage policy tolerates public traffic, and wire correct attribution. If any provider requiring a key is chosen instead, that key becomes the first browser-visible secret and security-checklist.md section 3 must be updated (the docs currently promise there are none).
acceptance_criteria: Production style loads from the chosen source within its usage policy; attribution is correct; either no token ships to the browser, or the docs are updated to reflect the first one.

title: Replay Source: run the app and tests from the recorded fixture
description: A Source implementation that replays the recorded aircraft.json fixture on a loop with advancing timestamps. Two reasons it must exist: the docs forbid testing against a live feeder, so ingest and stream tests need a deterministic Source; and bb dev should work on a laptop that cannot reach the home network. This is also the proof that the Source protocol seam actually holds.
acceptance_criteria: bb dev renders a moving map with no feeder reachable, driven by the replayed fixture; ingest and SSE tests consume the replay Source; replayed seen ages advance so staleness logic is exercised.

title: CI: run bb test and bb lint on every push
description: GitHub Actions workflow: JVM + babashka + Node setup, Playwright browser install for the CLJS suite, bb test and bb lint as required checks. The CLJS suite runs in real headless Chromium in CI exactly as locally - do not let CI quietly downgrade to jsdom.
acceptance_criteria: The workflow runs bb test and bb lint on push and PR; the CLJS suite runs in real headless Chromium in CI; a deliberately failing cljs test fails the build.
notes: CI must install Playwright browsers for the CLJS suite

title: Cloud-to-home tunnel for feeder connectivity
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the cloud backend reaches the home ultrafeeder over a VPN/tunnel (Tailscale, WireGuard, or cloudflared - pick one and document why). ADSB_ULTRAFEEDER_URL points at the tunnel address; the feeder is never exposed publicly. Verify boot-time URL validation and the poll-loop backoff behave sanely when the tunnel is down.
acceptance_criteria: The chosen tunnel is documented with rationale; the cloud container polls the home feeder over it; a downed tunnel behaves exactly like a feeder outage - backoff on the server, honest status in the UI; the feeder is reachable from nowhere else.
notes: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: cloudflared (Cloudflare Tunnel) from the home server. Pairs with DNS/TLS on Cloudflare if that is where the domain lands.

title: Production build: release CLJS + runnable jar
description: Make bb build real: shadow-cljs release build with optimizations, backend packaged as an uberjar (or clojure -X entry point) that serves the compiled frontend and reads all config from the environment. The artifact the Docker image will wrap.
acceptance_criteria: bb build produces an optimized CLJS release and a runnable jar that serves it; all config comes from the environment; the jar boots and serves the live map against a reachable feeder.

title: Dockerfile + compose for cloud deployment
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile (deps cache, build, slim JRE runtime), a compose file for the cloud host, health check wired to the health endpoint, and config via environment. Document the deploy loop in the README.
acceptance_criteria: The multi-stage image builds with a slim JRE runtime; compose up serves the app; the container healthcheck hits the health endpoint; the deploy loop is documented in the README.

title: Smooth interpolation between updates (dead reckoning)
description: Aircraft receive positions at ~1 Hz and must glide, not jump: between SSE ticks, project each positioned aircraft forward along track at ground speed via requestAnimationFrame, snapping to truth when a real update lands. Runs in the imperative map path - this must never touch React. Skip aircraft without gs/track; absent is not zero.
acceptance_criteria: Aircraft glide between ticks via rAF projection along track at ground speed and snap when a real update lands; aircraft without gs or track do not move; projection stops for stale aircraft; the map stays smooth.

title: Position trails with fading opacity
description: Per-aircraft position history kept client-side for the session (no persistence, per Overseer decision), rendered as a MapLibre line layer with opacity fading toward the tail. Cap history length per aircraft so memory is bounded; trails vanish when the aircraft ages out.
acceptance_criteria: A moving aircraft leaves a trail fading toward the tail; per-aircraft history is capped so memory is bounded; the trail is removed when the aircraft ages out; no React involvement.

title: Header, legend, connection status indicator
description: The frame around the map: app header, a legend explaining the altitude ramp and emergency treatment, aircraft count, and an honest connection indicator driven by the SSE connection sub - live, reconnecting, or feeder down. When the map is stale the user must be able to tell.
acceptance_criteria: The connection indicator honestly shows live / reconnecting / down from the SSE sub; the legend matches the real altitude ramp and emergency treatment; the aircraft count updates live.

title: Aircraft list sidebar with sort and filters
description: A Reagent sidebar listing current aircraft with sort (altitude, distance, callsign) and simple filters (e.g. positioned-only, airborne-only). Click-to-select syncs with the map. Driven by re-frame subs over the same aircraft state the map reads - one source of truth.
acceptance_criteria: The list sorts by altitude, distance, and callsign; filters (positioned-only, airborne-only) work; click-to-select syncs with the map; list and map read the same app-db state.

title: Client-side staleness: fade and remove silent aircraft
description: Aircraft that stop transmitting must visibly age: fade as seen-age grows, disappear past the age-out threshold, using the same shared stale? domain fn as the backend so the two sides never disagree about what stale means. The long-silent cast member is the acceptance test.
acceptance_criteria: The long-silent fixture fades as it ages and disappears past the threshold; both sides use the shared stale? fn; a selected aircraft that ages out clears the selection.

title: Aircraft selection + detail panel
description: Click an aircraft to select it: selection state in re-frame, a Reagent detail panel showing callsign, altitude, speed, track, squawk, seen-age, position - rendering absent fields as absent (a dash), never as zero. Selection survives SSE updates and clears when the aircraft ages out. Remember Boundary 4: every string in this panel came off an unauthenticated radio.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking an aircraft opens the panel; absent fields render as a dash, never zero; selection survives SSE ticks; the panel reads only re-frame subs.

title: Data-driven aircraft styling: heading rotation, altitude ramp, emergency state
description: MapLibre layer style expressions - not per-feature code: icon rotated by track, color ramped by altitude, distinct emergency treatment driven by feature properties. Altitude, heading, and emergency state are the data that carry meaning (AUTEUR); make them legible at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Icons rotate with track, color follows the altitude ramp, and emergency aircraft are visually distinct - all via style expressions over feature properties, no per-feature JS; the plane icon asset is registered in the style (sprite or addImage).

title: Plausibility layer: range gate + position-jump flagging
description: The second validation layer from validation-boundaries.md, beyond per-field limits: drop positions beyond the receiver horizon (needs receiver position - read it from ultrafeeder receiver.json or env), drop absurd fields while keeping the aircraft, and FLAG impossible position jumps rather than dropping them - a jump is the fingerprint of spoofing and must surface, not vanish. Requires prior state, so it sits on the state store.
acceptance_criteria: A position beyond the receiver horizon drops the aircraft; an absurd altitude or speed drops the field and keeps the aircraft; an impossible position jump is flagged, not dropped, and surfaces in the data; values are never silently clamped.
notes: Live probe shows the feeder emits r_dst / r_dir (receiver-relative distance and bearing) per aircraft - useful for the range gate server-side. PRIVACY (Overseer 2026-07-11): receiver position is hidden from the public site, and r_dst/r_dir must NEVER reach the wire format - one aircraft's position plus its r_dst/r_dir locates the antenna exactly. Strip them at ingest or at serialization, and test for their absence.

title: Cloud deployment and internet hardening
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the app runs in Docker in the cloud, internet-facing, reaching the home ultrafeeder over a VPN/tunnel (Tailscale/WireGuard/cloudflared class). Covers production build, image, tunnel connectivity, and the hardening that internet exposure demands beyond what the docs currently assume.
acceptance_criteria: The app runs in Docker in the cloud, internet-facing over TLS, fed from home over the tunnel, and holds up to anonymous traffic.

title: Polish features
description: The features that take the map from functional to finished, per Overseer scope decision 2026-07-11: position trails, dead-reckoning interpolation so aircraft glide instead of jump, enrichment from a static aircraft database, and receiver stats with range rings.
acceptance_criteria: Trails, smooth motion, enrichment, and receiver stats are live in production.

title: UI chrome
description: Everything around the map that IS Reagent/re-frame: selection and the detail panel, the aircraft list, header/legend/connection status, and the doc-mandated emergency-squawk alerting. Low-churn components driven by re-frame subs.
acceptance_criteria: The chrome is complete: select, inspect, list, filter, always know the connection state, and never miss an emergency squawk.

title: Build the fixture cast
description: test/cljc/adsb/fixtures.cljc — the six named aircraft from docs/testing-standards.md: ups-2717 (happy path), on-the-ground (alt_baro is the string), never-positioned (no lat/lon), squawking-7700 (emergency), long-silent (ages out), mlat-derived (lower confidence). Build them against the real Malli schemas so a schema change forces them to update.
acceptance_criteria: All six cast members exist in test/cljc/adsb/fixtures.cljc and validate against the real Malli schemas, so a schema change breaks the cast loudly; each member exhibits exactly the quirk it is named for.

title: Fill empty stack sections in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md
description: CLAUDE.md has empty placeholder sections: 'Build & Test', 'Architecture Overview', 'Conventions & Patterns'. Fill them with the decided stack and mirror into AGENTS.md per the repo's own note that the two files are independent and must be kept in sync.

title: Add MIT LICENSE
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the code is MIT licensed. Add the LICENSE file with the correct copyright holder and year, and reference it from the README.
acceptance_criteria: LICENSE file at repo root with MIT text and correct holder/year; README mentions the license.
close_reason: MIT LICENSE at repo root (Brandon Correa, 2026) + README license section; verified by host.

title: Receiver stats readout (no public receiver location)
description: Overseer decision 2026-07-11: the receiver position is private and must not be derivable from the public site - no range rings, no receiver marker, no antenna-centered default view. This bead is therefore a stats readout only: current aircraft count, positioned count, max observed range as a number, and message rate if the feeder exposes it. The backend still knows the true position for the range gate; it stays server-side.
acceptance_criteria: Stats show aircraft count, positioned count, max observed range, and message rate if available; nothing rendered or served reveals the receiver position; the map default view is a fixed general region, not the antenna.

title: Aircraft enrichment from a static aircraft database
description: Look up ICAO hex in a static database (tar1090-db style) to show type, registration, and operator in the detail panel and list. Decide where the DB lives (served by backend vs bundled) and treat it as data, not truth - enrichment must degrade to absent, never block the live path.
acceptance_criteria: A known hex shows type, registration, and operator in the panel and list; an unknown hex degrades to absent; the live path is unaffected when the DB is missing; the DB source and its license are documented.

title: Visual design pass against docs/design-direction.md
description: Once the Overseer settles the design direction (adsb-bvi.5) and the chrome exists, do the coherence pass: typography, spacing, color system, dark-map-appropriate palette, motion. AUTEUR proposes, Overseer disposes. This bead is the difference between assembled and designed.
acceptance_criteria: Typography, spacing, color, and motion cohere with docs/design-direction.md; the app is usable on a phone viewport; contrast and keyboard basics pass; Overseer signs off.

title: Decide the design direction
description: archetypes/AUTEUR.md previously referenced /docs/design-direction.md and /docs/design-questionnaire.md — both carried over from the old project and NEITHER EXISTS. Rather than invent the Overseer's taste, AUTEUR now says plainly that no direction is settled and instructs him to propose rather than assume. When the Overseer settles on one, write docs/design-direction.md and restore the reference in AUTEUR.
acceptance_criteria: docs/design-direction.md exists and records the Overseer's decision; the reference in archetypes/AUTEUR.md is restored; adsb-dgb.5 (visual design pass) is unblocked.
notes:
Overseer 2026-07-11: wants AUTEUR to propose 2-3 directions with mockups and will pick; no pre-existing taste recorded.

AUTEUR 2026-07-11 — three directions proposed, mockups in scratchpad design-directions/ (nocturne.html, sectional.html, terminal.html):

1) NOCTURNE — glass cockpit at night. Near-black blue map (#0B1220), glassmorphic chrome, data glows. Altitude ramp warm-to-cool: GND #6B7686 / low #FFB454 / mid #3FD9A4 / high #6FA8FF; accent cyan #46D8F0. System sans UI + tabular mono data. Aircraft: glowing chevrons w/ fading trails, continuous interpolation. Emergency #FF3B4E: pulsing expanding halo rings, red trail, pinned sidebar row, ribbon under header.

2) THE SECTIONAL — living aeronautical paper chart. Warm paper #F5EFDF, hypsometric terrain tints, contour lines, ink #2C2A24, aviation magenta #C0447C + aero blue #3D5E8C. Serif/italic editorial type, mono data blocks with leader lines like chart annotations. Aircraft: ink plan-view silhouettes, altitude via ink color (sienna #A65A2E low → magenta mid → blue high). Motion restrained; selection = dashed compass ring. Emergency #CE2029: hand-drawn red pen double-circle, rotated MAYDAY 7700 stamp, red NOTAM strip banner, hatched list row.

3) TERMINAL — airport wayfinding signage. Black #0C0C0C, signage yellow #FFCC00, Helvetica Neue heavy caps, departures-board sidebar, gate-sign detail bar. Basemap = white hairline coast/highway diagram only. Altitude as DISCRETE signage bands: GND gray / <10k yellow / 10-25k orange #FF7A00 / >25k white. Motion: split-flap ticks, snap-glide. Emergency #E10600: full-width red band flashing at 1 Hz like an apron beacon, aircraft becomes red square beacon, inverted board row.

All three honor: no receiver location/range rings anywhere; emergency unmissable; phone layouts included (media queries in mockups); chrome defers to map. No repo files touched; docs/design-direction.md deferred until the Overseer picks.
